title: Plungar Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 6 killed (1 survived); pilot Flt Sgt. Gerald Russell Avey

external_narratives.summary: IWM War Memorials Register entry for the Lancaster Bomber ED549 memorial at Plungar. Records the memorial as commemorating the crew lost when the aircraft crashed near the village on 5 March 1943.
key_details: Memorial catalogued in national War Memorials Register

external_narratives.summary: International crash site database entry for Lancaster ED549 at Plungar. Records the memorial at the crash site with details of the 100 Squadron aircraft lost during a mine-laying operation return.
key_details: Listed in international crash sites database
Aircraft was returning from mine-laying operation over France

external_narratives.summary: War Memorials Online listing for the Lancaster ED549 memorial at Plungar. Community-maintained record of the granite memorial stone unveiled on 22 September 2012 following a service at St Helen's Church.
key_details: Granite headstone-shaped stone with information board
Championed by local historian Tim Chamberlain

external_narratives.summary: County-level memorial register entry recording the RAF Lancaster Bomber crash memorial at Plungar, with local historical context for the Leicestershire area.
key_details: Listed in Leicestershire county memorial register

external_narratives.source_site: Air Crash Victims of World War II (tr4ce.co.uk)
external_narratives.summary: Detailed crew listing for Lancaster ED549 HW-S of 100 Squadron. Records crew ages: Flt Sgt Avey RCAF (21), Sgt Hallett (23), Sgt Spence (32), Sgt Cumberbatch (21), Sgt Robinson (34), Flt Sgt Landry RCAF (23), with Sgt D.S. Davies as the sole survivor.
key_details: Sole survivor was Sgt D.S. Davies

external_narratives.summary: Aviation accident database entry for Lancaster ED549. Records the aircraft attempted to land at Langar aerodrome in poor visibility but sank into ground in semi-stalled condition, aggravated by port inner engine failure and communication failure.
